title: Oil Exports and Revenues, Iran, 1963/64-1970/71 (World Bank, 1971)
source_org: International Bank for Reconstruction and Development (World Bank)
source_url: https://openknowledge.worldbank.org (World Bank Archives, Iran country documents)
extraction_method: MANUAL TRANSCRIPTION, not bulk download. Original document is a 1971 World Bank report on Iran's petroleum sector (data/raw/world-bank-archives-iran/historical-documents/1971_petroleum_sector.pdf, 89 pages) with a poor-quality OCR text layer (garbled digits and OCR noise, e.g. 'OIL EPXPORTS AND lREValUES_'). Table location was found via pdftotext -layout piped through a form-feed page split and searched for 'Table' headings, cross-checked against the document's own List of Tables (page 5) to identify Table 9 = 'Oil Exports and Revenues'. The exact page (PDF page 28, printed page 19) was rendered to a high-resolution PNG (pdftoppm -png -r 200 -f 28 -l 28) and every value was read directly from the image, which was fully legible (image much cleaner than the OCR text layer). No value was interpolated, estimated, or fabricated; dashes in the source table (no revenue in that year for that company) are preserved as blank cells.
time_coverage: 1963/64-1970/71 (Iranian calendar years; 1970/71 is a preliminary estimate)
units: see data.csv column headers / notes; export volumes in million metric tons, revenues in million US dollars, average revenue in US dollars per ton
license_terms: World Bank Archives — public disclosure authorized document
notes: Source document: data/raw/world-bank-archives-iran/historical-documents/1971_petroleum_sector.pdf — Table 9, PDF page 28 (printed page 19). Footnote 1/ in the source states export volumes refer to calendar year while revenue figures are by Iranian calendar year, but both are tabulated under the same year-column headings in the original -- reproduced as-is here. 'Consortium' = the Iranian Oil Participants consortium of international oil companies operating under the 1954 Agreement; 'NIOC' = National Iranian Oil Company (revenues from barter-trade crude exports); 'Other Companies' = joint-venture partners (ARAMCO-style SIRIP, IPAC, AGIP/ERAP etc.). Part of a systematic effort to extract Pahlavi-era (esp. 1960s-70s) data from primary-source World Bank PDFs already in data/raw/, extending the pre-1960 series already captured from the 1960 World Bank report.
